key_points: Housing costs rival tuition, sparking direct complaints to president
On-campus experience limited; school functions primarily as commuter school
Snow/ice events expose how campus fails non-residential students
key_quotes: big G better run before I ask him why housing is the same price as my tuition
I am so done with Mason. It's literally a commuter school, yet they act as if everyone lives on campus
Are you kidding me...for a commuter school...

key_points: Campus social scene is genuinely thin; commuter culture kills community
Greek life exists but most students ignore it entirely
Quirky campus culture—robots, igloos, geese—substitutes for traditional social events
key_quotes: There are fraternities that dedicate their efforts...And yet this goes completely disregarded by most students
Feedback is almost identical from when I went there 30 years ago. Not your traditional on campus experience
I love attending Mason for a number of reasons, and these stupid little robots are embarrassingly high on the list

key_points: Mouse spotted walking through Ike's dining hall on camera
Food quality at Ike's draws mockery; late-night options are mediocre
Starship robot delivery is a genuine campus highlight students love
key_quotes: YIke's
Still not the worst thing in any Mason dining establishment; throwback to the maggots in Chipotle back in 2018
but late night ike's when that older woman is on the grill…delectable

key_points: Exam stress is intense; students found passed out on library floors
Professors occasionally show genuine compassion—rounding up grades, giving breaks
No strong mental health support culture mentioned; students cope individually
key_quotes: I cannot believe how kind my professor is! I've never had someone truly give me a break like this before
Had a prof not round up 89.97... Ya boy was in rehab LMAOOO
I missed my college days, you won't have such experience anymore once you're out of school

key_points: In-state tuition is a major draw, especially vs. DC-area private schools
Housing costs described as equal to tuition—a serious financial burden
Common advice: start at NVCC, transfer to Mason, save significant money
key_quotes: I just got in today with a distinction scholarship and in the honors college!
I am currently going to Northern Virginia Community College looking to transfer to George Mason University through the Guaranteed Admission Agreement
If I had to go back and do it all over again, I would have started at NVCC and transferred

key_points: Cheating is a visible, recurring problem in CS and physics classes
AI tools are actively disrupting exam integrity; professors are frustrated
Students built tools to find easy-grading professors—grade inflation is real
key_quotes: Imagine cheating on a 200 level course at GMU
U have to see people with the AI meta glasses during the exam
I am continuously surprised at how the idiocy of this university brings out the best in us

key_points: Admin kept campus open during dangerous ice, students were injured falling
Graduation processing error: student walked the stage but never officially graduated
Snow day decisions made absurdly late—midnight parking emails, 8am closures
key_quotes: Damn I knew GMU does shit last minute but this is crazy
apparently I never graduated... I had submitted for my graduation in the winter semester and was pre approved
I was hoping GMU would have improved in the ten years since I was a student re: snow days

key_points: Fairfax is suburban and quiet, but DC is only 30 min by Metro
Campus infrastructure is aging—rusty railings, icy uncleared sidewalks regularly
Campus has genuine charm: pond, wildlife, observatory, snow igloos
key_quotes: if you like the city feel but dont like being in traffic all the time fairfax is good because its usually just a 30 min metro ride into DC
Sad to see the campus falling apart—a lot of the other railings are rusty too
Oh nah that lake is crazy

key_points: Entry-level salaries start low (~$34k) but job-hopping brings rapid growth
One alum: $34k first job, $110k four years later via job-hopping
Some graduates report unemployment a year out; major matters enormously
key_quotes: I actually went to this school, first job after graduation was $34k, 4 years later I'm making $110k, job hopping guy is spot on
I graduated GMU a year ago, and wanted to report I'm still unemployed
tons of opportunities if you're willing to put in some time and effort

key_points: In-state tuition plus DC-area job market makes GMU a solid value
Housing costs eating into that value; president's salary over $800k draws ire
NOVA-to-Mason pipeline is widely recommended as the smartest financial path
key_quotes: Public reminder Gregory's salary is over 800k meanwhile we have hundreds and hundreds of issues with campus
All of my friendships from the first two years fell apart anyways, and it would have saved me a ton of money
instate tuition makes a 20k difference

key_points: Underdog, scrappy identity—students are proud but unsentimental about GMU
Diverse, commuter-heavy, practical-minded student body; not a traditional college experience
Genuine community warmth exists despite the chaos—people help each other
key_quotes: Never knew attending GMU would be such an act of revolution but I'm proudly here for it!
You're not behind, you're just taking the scenic route
This is my dream college, it's soo diverse and it's so beautiful seeing people from all over the world just uniting

key_points: **Safety**: Admin kept campus open during ice storms; student crushed elbow falling
**Sexual misconduct**: Upskirt photo attempt reported on campus; predator with scholarship arrested
**Administration**: Student officially never graduated despite walking the stage and getting congratulatory emails
key_quotes: Crushed my elbow on the way to class... Genuinely why are we open? There are huge patches of ice
A guy pointed his phone at me and tried to take an inappropriate picture of me without my consent
apparently I never graduated... I walked the stage and celebrated with my family as a first generation graduate

key_points: Campus observatory offers free public nights with a massive 32-inch telescope
Starship robot delivery has genuine cult following—students genuinely love them
Notable alumni: Golden Globe winner Rhea Seehorn, Olympian Ilia Malinin, NBA champion Tyler Kolek
key_quotes: It's a nice Ritchey-Chrétien Cassigrain reflector. It's about 32 inches, which is really huge
I love attending Mason for a number of reasons, and these stupid little robots are embarrassingly high on the list
GMU now has the Best Actress and World Champion male figure skater at the same time
